meta.title: The Eastern Orthodox Doctrine of Justification
meta.author: Pastor Joshua Schooping

questions.question: Which council’s decrees does the speaker chiefly cite for the Eastern Orthodox doctrine of justification?
questions.answer: The Jerusalem Council of 1672

questions.question: Whose Protestant-leaning confession does the speaker say the Jerusalem Council of 1672 refuted chapter by chapter?
questions.answer: Cyril Lucaris

questions.question: In what year does the speaker date Lucaris’s confession?
Which recent pan-Orthodox gathering does the speaker cite as reaffirming authority for later “universal” councils after the seventh ecumenical council?
options: The Holy and Great Council in Crete (2016)
questions.answer: The Holy and Great Council in Crete (2016)

questions.question: According to the speaker’s reading of Jerusalem Decree 9, faith is defined as what?
options: Fiducial trust in Christ’s merits alone
The right notion in us concerning God and divine things, working by love
Private judgment of Scripture without the church
A temporary gift that may be lost without repentance
questions.answer: The right notion in us concerning God and divine things, working by love

questions.question: In Lucaris chapter 9 (as summarized), what is said to justify the believer?
options: Keeping the divine commandments without faith
Faith which justifies in Christ Jesus, grounded in Christ’s life and death and the gospel
questions.answer: Faith which justifies in Christ Jesus, grounded in Christ’s life and death and the gospel

questions.question: Which Latin term does the speaker use for intellectual knowledge or “right ideas” as one aspect of faith?
questions.answer: Notitia

questions.question: Which term does the speaker use for heart-level entrustment and commitment in saving faith?
questions.answer: Fiducia

questions.question: Which Lutheran theologian does the speaker mention alongside a fourfold ordering that includes desiderium?
questions.answer: Martin Chemnitz

questions.question: In Lucaris chapter 13 (as summarized), what does the speaker identify as the correlative or object of justifying faith?
options: The righteousness of Christ apprehended and applied to the penitent
The believer’s own virtues accumulated over a lifetime
The authority of the patriarch alone
questions.answer: The righteousness of Christ apprehended and applied to the penitent

questions.question: According to the speaker’s quotation of Jerusalem Decree 13, how is a person justified?
options: Not simply through faith alone but through faith working through love—faith and works
By philosophical knowledge of the Trinity only
By monastic tonsure regardless of faith
questions.answer: Not simply through faith alone but through faith working through love—faith and works

questions.question: What image does Decree 13 reject regarding faith and Christ’s righteousness?
options: Faith as a sacrifice offered daily
Faith as a hand that lays hold on righteousness in Christ and applies it for salvation
Faith as identical to baptismal water
questions.answer: Faith as a hand that lays hold on righteousness in Christ and applies it for salvation

questions.question: Why does Decree 13 (as reported) call the rejected “hand” notion of faith “far from all Orthodoxy”?
options: Because it would make faith possible for all, so none could miss salvation—which they say is false
Because it forbids good works entirely
Because it requires faith in Mary as co-redemptrix
questions.answer: Because it would make faith possible for all, so none could miss salvation—which they say is false

questions.question: According to the speaker’s reading of Decree 13, what justifies us instead of the correlative of faith?
options: The faith which is in us, through works with Christ
questions.answer: The faith which is in us, through works with Christ

questions.question: How does Decree 13 characterize works, according to the speaker?
options: As fruits through which faith becomes efficacious and as meriting through divine promises
questions.answer: As fruits through which faith becomes efficacious and as meriting through divine promises

questions.question: Which Pauline verse does the speaker cite to stress personal appropriation (“for me”) of Christ’s love?
questions.answer: Galatians 2:20

questions.question: Which figure is named with councils in 1341, 1351, and 1368 in the speaker’s list?
questions.answer: Gregory Palamas

questions.question: The speaker mentions 1484 in Constantinople in connection with refuting which unionist council?
questions.answer: Florence

questions.question: What does the speaker argue is the positional problem if one appeals at judgment to one’s own faith and works rather than Christ’s righteousness?
options: It is a form of self-righteousness (positional, not merely psychological boasting)
It eliminates the need for the church
questions.answer: It is a form of self-righteousness (positional, not merely psychological boasting)

questions.question: How does the speaker summarize the Protestant comfort regarding weak faith?
options: Weak faith receives a strong Savior because confidence rests in Christ’s objective work
Weak faith proves one was never elect
Weak faith must be hidden from the pastor
Weak faith is stronger than strong faith
questions.answer: Weak faith receives a strong Savior because confidence rests in Christ’s objective work

questions.question: Which patristic figures does the speaker name as not teaching (in his view) what Jerusalem 1672 commits to on justification?
options: John Chrysostom, Cyril of Alexandria, and Athanasius
Irenaeus, Justin Martyr, and Origen only
questions.answer: John Chrysostom, Cyril of Alexandria, and Athanasius

questions.question: What ecclesiological heresy does the speaker associate with an 1872 council in his timeline?
questions.answer: Ethnophyletism

questions.question: Which city hosted a 1691 council that the speaker says named and affirmed Jerusalem 1672?
questions.answer: Constantinople

questions.question: Regarding Christus Victor, what critique does the speaker offer in connection with Orthodox justification as he reads it?
options: Christ’s victory makes self-salvation possible through right notions plus works rather than atoning rest in Christ’s righteousness
Christus Victor was invented by Calvin
questions.answer: Christ’s victory makes self-salvation possible through right notions plus works rather than atoning rest in Christ’s righteousness
